2605.14678.title: $π$-Bench: Evaluating Proactive Personal Assistant Agents in Long-Horizon Workflows
2605.14678.paper_summary: The rise of personal assistant agents, e.g., OpenClaw, highlights the growing potential of large language models to support users across everyday life and work. A core challenge in these settings is proactive assistance, since users often begin with underspecified requests and leave important needs, constraints, or preferences unstated. However, existing benchmarks rarely evaluate whether agents can identify and act on such hidden intents before they are explicitly stated, especially in sustained multi-turn interactions where user needs emerge gradually. To address this gap, we introduce $π$-Bench, a benchmark for proactive assistance comprising 100 multi-turn tasks across 5 domain-specific user personas. By incorporating hidden user intents, inter-task dependencies, and cross-session continuity, $π$-Bench evaluates agents' ability to anticipate and address user needs over extended interactions, jointly measuring proactivity and task completion in long-horizon trajectories that better reflect real-world use. Experiments show (1) proactive assistance remains challenging, (2) a clear distinction between task completion and proactivity, and (3) the value of prior interaction for proactive intent resolution in later tasks.
2605.14678.paper_summary_zh: 個人助理代理的興起，例如 OpenClaw，突顯了大型語言模型在日常生活和工作中支持用戶的潛力。<lb/>這些環境中的一個核心挑戰是主動協助，因為用戶通常以不明確的請求開始，並且未明言重要的需求、限制或偏好。<lb/>然而，現有的基準很少評估代理是否能夠在這些隱藏的意圖被明確表達之前識別並採取行動，特別是在持續的多輪互動中，用戶需求逐漸顯現。<lb/>為了填補這一空白，我們介紹了 $π$-Bench，一個針對主動協助的基準，包含 100 個涉及 5 種特定領域用戶角色的多輪任務。<lb/>通過納入隱藏的用戶意圖、任務之間的依賴關係和跨會話的連貫性，$π$-Bench 評估代理在延長互動中預測和滿足用戶需求的能力，並共同測量在更好反映現實世界使用情境的長期軌跡中的主動性和任務完成度。<lb/>實驗顯示 (1) 主動協助仍然具有挑戰性，(2) 任務完成和主動性之間存在明確區分，以及 (3) 先前互動對於後續任務中主動意圖解決的價值。
2605.14678.author: Haoran Zhang et.al.
2605.14678.authors: Haoran Zhang, Luxin Xu, Zhilin Wang, Runquan Gui, Shunkai Zhang, Haodi Lei, Zihao He, Bingsu He, Chicheng Qin, Tong Zhu, Xiaoye Qu, Yang Yang, Yu Cheng, Yafu Li
